claim: Reusable scientific memory should not be promoted unless downstream claims can be audited and downgraded after revocation.

revocation_route.revocation_target: durable reusable memory packet and derived retrieval index entry
revocation_route.required_actor: memory curator or automated policy gate
revocation_route.verification_signal: packet is absent from active retrieval and all dependent packets show downgraded status

redaction_route.semantic_loss_note: Redaction may preserve governance metadata while removing biological or personal-context semantics.

downgrade_route.revalidation_trigger: new source evidence, renewed consent, or successful dependency reconstruction test

downstream_dependencies.impact_if_removed: promotion must pause until the claim is rebuilt from remaining evidence

missingness: hidden cache copies are not yet exhaustively detectable
no live benchmark yet scores privacy-risk reduction after revocation
revision_reason: Adds safe removal and downstream downgrade semantics to prior promotion-focused gates.
falsification_route: Run a dependency-removal simulation; if dependent claims remain promoted or retrieval still exposes revoked content, the gate fails.
next_executable_action: Run the regression test and add this gate to the discovery-memory promotion pipeline.
